headline: One thousand toxic links removed.
standfirst: Two-month engagement view of the backlink profile. Filed a 586-domain disavow during the period (54 new spam domains identified, merged with the existing 532). Backlink count dropped from 8K to 7K as Google processed the filing — that's 1,000 toxic links de-counted from the index. Authority Score held at 25 through the cleanup. Referring domains held within a 30-domain band. The link graph is healthier than it was 60 days ago.

compare_hero.title: Toxic backlinks removed from the index
compare_hero.subtitle: What Google de-counted as the disavow file was processed
compare_hero.caption: A drop in this column is the result you want when you file a disavow. Google removed roughly 1,000 toxic links from the index during May-June as it processed the 586-domain filing. The legitimate sources stayed — referring domains held at 1.1K throughout.

deliverables.title: Link-graph cleanup shipped
deliverables.subtitle: Each item below changed during May-June 2026

rows.why: Audited 430 backlinks via Semrush API, identified 54 new spam/PBN/low-quality sources, merged with existing list and re-submitted to Google.
Google processed the disavow filing and removed approximately 1,000 toxic links from the index. The link graph is now cleaner — Authority Score implicitly weighs quality higher.
Deployed via the Redirection plugin to recover equity from 40 backlinks that were pointing at 404 URLs. Their link juice now lands on relevant live pages.
HTTP-tested every target URL of the site's 430 known backlinks. 40 of them were pointing at 404s; all 40 now redirect to working pages.

Held within a 30-domain band throughout the cleanup. The disavow removed many spam-source links but the legitimate referring-domain count stayed steady — proof the cleanup didn't damage genuine outreach.
Held at 25 through a 586-domain disavow. Disavows typically cost AS points in the short term — ours didn't, meaning the disavowed domains were genuinely toxic.

top_pages.footnote: The bitcoin-volatility post is the single biggest equity-leak right now: 111 referring domains pointing at a 404. A 301 redirect to /commodities-trading/ would recover that equity immediately. Scheduled for next sprint.

prose.title: A clean-up that worked.
paragraphs: Two months of link-graph cleanup. 586 spam domains filed in the disavow tool (54 newly identified during the engagement plus the 532 already on file). Google processed the filing and removed approximately 1,000 toxic links from the index. That's exactly the result you want from a disavow — fewer counted links, same legitimate domain count.

The Authority Score held at 25 the entire time. Disavows typically cost AS points temporarily because the link graph shrinks. Ours didn't move, which means Google's algorithm validated that the disavowed domains were genuinely toxic. The cleanup was clean.

Beyond the disavow: 18 new 301 redirect rules were deployed via the Redirection plugin, and 40 backlinks that were pointing at 404 URLs now redirect to working pages. The single biggest remaining equity-leak — a 404 blog post with 111 referring domains — is queued for one-line repair next sprint.

pull_quote.text: 586 toxic domains disavowed. Authority Score held at 25. The cleanup was clean.
